# Semantic HTML Reference

Use this reference when creating or restructuring markup.
Choose elements based on meaning and behavior, not appearance.

---

## Element Decision Guide

### Interactive Elements

| Intent | Element | Not This |
|---|---|---|
| Trigger an action | `<button>` | `<div onClick>` |
| Navigate to a URL | `<a href="...">` | `<button onClick={navigate}>` |
| Submit data | `<form>` + `<button type="submit">` | `<div>` + `<button onClick={submit}>` |
| Toggle a boolean | `<input type="checkbox">` | `<div onClick={toggle}>` |
| Choose one of many | `<input type="radio">` | custom `<div>` toggles |
| Select from a list | `<select>` | custom dropdown (unless a11y is handled) |

### Page Structure

| Purpose | Element |
|---|---|
| Primary content | `<main>` (one per page) |
| Site navigation | `<nav>` |
| Page or section header | `<header>` |
| Page or section footer | `<footer>` |
| Thematic section | `<section>` (with heading) |
| Self-contained content | `<article>` |
| Tangentially related | `<aside>` |

### Content Elements

| Purpose | Element |
|---|---|
| Heading hierarchy | `<h1>` through `<h6>` |
| Paragraph | `<p>` |
| Ordered steps | `<ol>` + `<li>` |
| Unordered items | `<ul>` + `<li>` |
| Key-value pairs | `<dl>` + `<dt>` + `<dd>` |
| Tabular data | `<table>` + `<thead>` + `<tbody>` + `<th>` + `<td>` |
| Quotation | `<blockquote>` or `<q>` |
| Code | `<code>`, `<pre>` |
| Time or date | `<time datetime="...">` |
| Abbreviation | `<abbr title="...">` |
| Figure with caption | `<figure>` + `<figcaption>` |

### Form Elements

| Purpose | Element |
|---|---|
| Group related fields | `<fieldset>` + `<legend>` |
| Label an input | `<label for="...">` or wrapping `<label>` |
| Text input | `<input type="text">` |
| Multi-line text | `<textarea>` |
| Output of calculation | `<output>` |
| Progress indicator | `<progress>` |
| Measurement within a range | `<meter>` |

### Interactive Elements (Native)

| Purpose | Element |
|---|---|
| Toggle disclosure | `<details>` + `<summary>` |
| Modal / dialog | `<dialog>` |
| Search landmark | `<search>` |

---

## Heading Hierarchy

Headings must follow a logical hierarchy. Never skip levels for styling.

```html
<!-- Correct -->
<h1>Dashboard</h1>
  <h2>Recent Activity</h2>
    <h3>Today</h3>
    <h3>Yesterday</h3>
  <h2>Statistics</h2>

<!-- Wrong — skips h2 -->
<h1>Dashboard</h1>
  <h3>Recent Activity</h3>
```

Rules:
- One `<h1>` per page (the page title).
- Each section should start with the next heading level.
- Never choose a heading level for its font size — use CSS.

---

## Landmark Regions

Landmark elements create navigable regions for assistive technology.

```html
<body>
  <header>
    <nav aria-label="Main">...</nav>
  </header>

  <main>
    <section aria-labelledby="overview-heading">
      <h2 id="overview-heading">Overview</h2>
      ...
    </section>
  </main>

  <aside aria-label="Related links">...</aside>

  <footer>...</footer>
</body>
```

Rules:
- Use `aria-label` or `aria-labelledby` when multiple landmarks of the same type exist.
- `<main>` must appear only once.
- `<nav>` should have an accessible name if more than one exists on the page.

---

## Lists

Use the correct list type for the content.

```html
<!-- Steps (order matters) -->
<ol>
  <li>Select a plan</li>
  <li>Enter payment details</li>
  <li>Confirm subscription</li>
</ol>

<!-- Features (order irrelevant) -->
<ul>
  <li>Unlimited storage</li>
  <li>Priority support</li>
  <li>Custom domain</li>
</ul>

<!-- Metadata / key-value -->
<dl>
  <dt>Status</dt>
  <dd>Active</dd>
  <dt>Created</dt>
  <dd><time datetime="2026-01-15">January 15, 2026</time></dd>
</dl>
```

---

## Tables

Use tables only for tabular data. Never for layout.

```html
<table>
  <caption>Monthly revenue by region</caption>
  <thead>
    <tr>
      <th scope="col">Region</th>
      <th scope="col">Revenue</th>
      <th scope="col">Growth</th>
    </tr>
  </thead>
  <tbody>
    <tr>
      <th scope="row">North America</th>
      <td>$1.2M</td>
      <td>+12%</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <th scope="row">Europe</th>
      <td>$850K</td>
      <td>+8%</td>
    </tr>
  </tbody>
</table>
```

Rules:
- Use `<caption>` to describe the table.
- Use `scope="col"` and `scope="row"` on `<th>` elements.
- Use `<thead>` and `<tbody>` to group rows.

---

## Forms

```html
<form aria-labelledby="contact-heading">
  <h2 id="contact-heading">Contact Us</h2>

  <fieldset>
    <legend>Your Information</legend>

    <div>
      <label for="name">Full Name</label>
      <input id="name" name="name" type="text" required autocomplete="name" />
    </div>

    <div>
      <label for="email">Email Address</label>
      <input id="email" name="email" type="email" required autocomplete="email" />
    </div>
  </fieldset>

  <fieldset>
    <legend>Inquiry Type</legend>
    <label>
      <input type="radio" name="type" value="support" />
      Support
    </label>
    <label>
      <input type="radio" name="type" value="sales" />
      Sales
    </label>
  </fieldset>

  <div>
    <label for="message">Message</label>
    <textarea id="message" name="message" required></textarea>
  </div>

  <button type="submit">Send Message</button>
</form>
```

Rules:
- Every input must have a visible `<label>`.
- Group related inputs with `<fieldset>` and `<legend>`.
- Use appropriate `type` attributes (`email`, `tel`, `url`, `number`, `date`).
- Use `autocomplete` attributes for common fields.
- Use `required` for mandatory fields.

---

## When `div` and `span` Are Fine

Use `div` and `span` when:
- The element is purely for styling or layout (flex container, grid wrapper).
- No semantic element matches the purpose.
- The content is inline text decoration (`span`) within a meaningful parent.

```html
<!-- div as layout container — fine -->
<div className="grid grid-cols-3 gap-4">
  <article>...</article>
  <article>...</article>
  <article>...</article>
</div>

<!-- span for inline styling — fine -->
<p>Your balance is <span className="font-bold text-green-600">$1,234</span></p>
```

If you're adding `role`, `tabIndex`, `onClick`, or `onKeyDown` to a `div`, you almost certainly want a semantic element instead.

---

## Native `<dialog>`

Use the native `<dialog>` element instead of building custom modals from scratch.

```html
<!-- Non-modal dialog (popover-like) -->
<dialog id="info-dialog">
  <p>Additional information here.</p>
  <button onclick="this.closest('dialog').close()">Close</button>
</dialog>

<!-- Modal dialog (blocks background, traps focus, handles Escape) -->
<dialog id="confirm-dialog">
  <h2>Confirm deletion</h2>
  <p>This action cannot be undone.</p>
  <form method="dialog">
    <button value="cancel">Cancel</button>
    <button value="confirm">Delete</button>
  </form>
</dialog>
```

Why native `<dialog>`:
- `showModal()` traps focus, adds `::backdrop`, makes background inert.
- `Escape` closes it automatically.
- `method="dialog"` on a form closes the dialog and returns the button's value.
- No need for portal, focus trap library, or scroll lock.

---

## Native `<details>` / `<summary>`

Use for disclosure widgets (accordion, expandable sections) before reaching for JavaScript.

```html
<details>
  <summary>How do I reset my password?</summary>
  <p>Go to Settings → Security → Reset Password.</p>
</details>

<!-- Open by default -->
<details open>
  <summary>Advanced Options</summary>
  <fieldset>
    <legend>Export format</legend>
    <label><input type="radio" name="format" value="csv" /> CSV</label>
    <label><input type="radio" name="format" value="json" /> JSON</label>
  </fieldset>
</details>
```

The `toggle` event fires when the state changes, useful for tracking or animation:
```tsx
<details onToggle={(e) => {
  const open = (e.currentTarget as HTMLDetailsElement).open;
  trackEvent("faq_toggle", { open });
}}>
```

---

## `<search>` Element

Wraps a set of elements used for searching or filtering. Replaces `<div role="search">`.

```html
<search>
  <form action="/search">
    <label for="q">Search articles</label>
    <input id="q" name="q" type="search" />
    <button type="submit">Search</button>
  </form>
</search>
```

---

## Text-Level Semantics

Elements that are often overlooked but carry meaning:

```html
<!-- Highlighted / marked text -->
<p>Search results for "react": <mark>React</mark> is a JavaScript library.</p>

<!-- Deleted and inserted content (edits, diffs) -->
<p>Price: <del>$49</del> <ins>$29</ins></p>

<!-- Abbreviation with expansion -->
<p>The <abbr title="World Wide Web Consortium">W3C</abbr> maintains web standards.</p>

<!-- Machine-readable time -->
<p>Published <time datetime="2026-04-16">April 16, 2026</time></p>

<!-- Measurement within a known range -->
<label for="disk">Disk usage:</label>
<meter id="disk" value="0.7" min="0" max="1" low="0.3" high="0.8" optimum="0.5">70%</meter>
```
